title: CI-enforced supply-chain gate with CycloneDX SBOM generation
source: OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025) — LLM03 Supply Chain; codebase: supply_chain_check() (security_utils.py:1326) runs pip-audit but is never enforced as a gate or paired with an SBOM
description: supply_chain_check() already runs pip-audit against a hash-pinned requirements.lock, but nothing enforces it in CI and there's no Software Bill of Materials artifact. OWASP LLM03 calls for continuous dependency vulnerability gating plus an SBOM for provenance/auditability. Wire the existing function into a failing CI gate and emit a CycloneDX SBOM per build.
proposed_changes: Add a small CLI entrypoint (e.g. `python scripts/security_utils.py --supply-chain`) that calls supply_chain_check() and exits non-zero on status=='vulnerable'. Create .github/workflows/security.yml that installs pip-audit + cyclonedx-bom, runs the gate on PRs/push, generates a CycloneDX SBOM (`cyclonedx-py requirements requirements.lock -o sbom.json`), and uploads it as a build artifact. Extend supply_chain_check() to also verify the lockfile is hash-pinned (already uses --require-hashes) and to accept a `fail_on` severity threshold so low-severity CVEs can be triaged without blocking every build.
